description: A real candid wide-angle photograph of the U.S. Department of Labor (Frances Perkins Building) facade in Washington, D.C. under a bright blue sky with scattered fluffy clouds. Two large maroon vertical banners hang on the building, each showing a portrait and the text 'AMERICA 250' and 'AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST': the left banner depicts President Trump, and the right banner depicts a historical figure (Theodore Roosevelt). A large American flag is draped vertically between them, and U.S. and other flags fly from poles. The building name 'FRANCES PERKINS / DEPARTMENT OF LABOR BUILDING' is engraved on the stone base, and a small figure photographs the display from the steps below. The tweet caption ('some are calling this the greatest glow up of all time') promotes the agency's Trump-themed redecoration of its headquarters. The image itself is an ordinary photograph without added graphic-design overlays, so is_designed=false.
notable_text: AMERICAN WORKERS FIRST; AMERICA 250 (on the building banners)
